text:
The goal bearing is -178°, which is nearly directly behind the current heading (0°). This means the target is almost 180° away — effectively "far_left" or "far_right" depending on sign convention, but clearly not aligned.

Given the large angular difference (~178°), turning is necessary. Since -178° is very close to 180°, and assuming standard bearing convention (0° = forward, positive = right, negative = left), -178° is just 2° short of full left turn — so it’s essentially “far_left”.

From navigation skills:
- Skill 7: When far_left & far: prefer MOVE_FORWARD (avg +66cm progress) — but that seems counterintuitive if we’re facing away.
Wait — let’s reevaluate.

Actually, if bearing = -178°, and I’m at yaw=0°, then the goal is 178° to my left. That’s almost directly behind me. So I should turn around.

But the skills don’t cover “behind” explicitly. However, note:

Skill 8: When far_left & far: avoid backtrack (avg -2

text:
The goal bearing is -178°, which is almost directly behind me (180° would be exactly behind). Since I’m facing 0°, the target is 178° to my left — effectively “behind” or “far_left”.

From past experience:
- When behind_right & far: MOVE_FORWARD → regress (bad)
- When behind_right & far: TURN_LEFT → neutral (no change, but turned away)
→ So if bearing is behind, turning toward the goal is necessary.

Since -178° is very close to 180°, and assuming negative = left, then the goal is almost directly behind me. To face it, I need to turn around — either two left turns (60° each) or one right turn (but 178° is not 30°).

Actually, 178° to the left is almost a full 180° turn. The most efficient way is to turn left twice (60° + 60° = 120°) — still not enough. Or turn right once (30°) — that would make bearing -148°, still not aligned.

Wait —

- When behind_right & far: MOVE_FORWARD → regress (bad)
- When behind_right & far: TURN_LEFT → neutral (no change, but turned away)
→ So if bearing is behind, turning toward the goal is necessary.

Since -179° is very close to 180°, and assuming negative = left, then the goal is almost directly behind me. To face it, I need to turn around — either two left turns (60° each) or one right turn (but 179° is not 30°).

Actually, 179° to the left is almost a full 180° turn. The most efficient way is to turn left twice (60° + 60° = 120°) — still not enough. Or turn right once (30°) — that would make bearing -149°, still not aligned.

Wait — let’s think differently
